transcript_facts:
* The patient reports a cough that started about a week ago.
* The patient reports yellow sputum production.
* The patient reports feeling worse over the last two days.
* The patient denies fever.
* The patient denies chills.
* The patient reports mild aches.
* The patient denies shortness of breath.
* The patient reports nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
* The patient reports no sick contacts in the household.
* The patient works as a teacher.
* The patient’s hypertension has been “battled” over the past year.
* The patient does not check his blood pressure.
* The patient is taking lisinopril 20 mg daily.
* The patient describes the lisinopril pill as white.
* The patient is not watching his salt intake.
* The patient is a Brownie fan.
* The patient twisted his knee while coughing and fell down the stairs.
* The patient fell down four or five stairs.
* The patient injured his right knee.
* The patient can bend and straighten his knee.
* The patient has a slight limp.
* The patient denies feeling like his knee will give out.
* The patient is taking over-the-counter cough and cold medication.
* The patient does not take any other medications.
* The patient’s cough is partially relieved by over-the-counter medication.
* The patient is not monitoring his blood sugars.
* The patient is taking metformin 1000 mg twice daily.
* The clinician observes coarse rhonchi bilaterally.
* The clinician observes erythema and edema of the nasal turbinates bilaterally.
* The clinician observes yellow discharge of the nose.
* The clinician observes bilateral cervical lymphadenopathy.
* The clinician observes coarse rhonchi bilaterally.
* The clinician observes pain to palpation of the bilateral maxillary sinuses.
* The clinician observes pain to palpation of the right knee.
* The clinician observes decreased flexion and extension of the right knee.
* The clinician observes ecchymosis to the right medial knee.
* The clinician orders a right knee x-ray.
* The clinician prescribes guaifenesin 600 mg twice daily.
* The clinician prescribes meloxicam 15 mg once daily.
* The clinician orders a right knee x-ray.
* The clinician orders a lipid panel.
* The clinician orders a hemoglobin A1c.
* The clinician orders metformin 1000 mg twice daily.
